opp.name: Changi Airport_S2P
opp.next_step: 50% Confidence 6th Jun- evaluation has slowed while ME situation improves May 11- no update- KIV 4th May- sent 1:1 email to Wun Wai Apr 27 Not able to re-ngage. Feedback from partner is the same. Changi became silent as working internally on RFP (expected by mid-year). Invitation to RT with no confirmation so far Apr 13: working internally on RFP to be delivered by June. Became silent. KIV. Collaterals & RT invitation Mar 13- weekly engagement but no clarification on next steps so far. Same feedback from Kpmg. Mar03- Accenture confirms CAG should go to market in June/July (as SAP migration is now on track). Feb 04 Positive meeting with strong possibility to be part of short list (RFP in second half of 2026). Action to engage with CFO to get an executive buying based on superior AI capabilities. Still need to work on mitigating previous experience. Feb 02 meeting with IT & procurement on Feb 04...I2O focus to lead on innovation and mitigate previous experience...current objective is to be short listed. Dec 10 Meeting with CPO confirmed by Jan 16 Dec 08th Meeting with procurement leaders on Jan 16 Nov 28th Message to Mun Wai for F2F Nov24th: No update so far- main focus is on S4H migration KO. 5th Nov Reached out for a meeting with Mun Wai. Plan to overview the "new Zycus" and review of STE selection etc 30th Oct: CFO exec meeting by mid of Nov. 13rd Oct: Customer responded positively to re engage with Zycus. Carl pending of meeting with CFO (Mun Wai). 16th Sep: Partner (KPMG) reconfirmed- evaluation expected to begin in 2025- award early 2026. Key stakeholder Mun Wai Mak, is not favoring Zycus, but is open to meet and hear proposition/pitch. Action- plan exec cover (ck) create win theme/strategy to rebuild confidence and leverage STE & NTUC as selecting and trusting Zycus 12nd Sep: Zycus not well positioned because of past project. Working on re engaging with C Level 14th Jul: Will launch a BOFU action by August 23rd Jun: Confirmation RFP will come in Sept. To work on BOFU with marketing. Checking with partners with which vendors they will respond. 16th Jun: Dedicated RFP for procurement potentially expected by Sep (to align with partners Accenture, Kpmg so far). BD to share collaterals. Sales to work on executive engagement. 15th May: No confirmation around any S2P initiative. KIV BD 19th Mar: ERP migration RFP shared on Mar 07. To clarify if procurement is part of scope else which is the alternative. 09th Dec: RFP to be launched in Jan 25 (to clarify if standalone or part of ERP one). Currently doing a POC with a partner. 02nd Dec: SIs are invited to share best practice on Gen AI use cases. Zycus partnering with Kpmg. Expect RFP by Q1 25.
opp.ais_why:
• Per the 4 Feb 2026 rep note from Guillaume Pasquet: 'Positive meeting with strong possibility to be part of short list (RFP in second half of 2026). Action to engage with CFO to get an executive buying based on superior AI capabilities. Still need to work on mitigating previous experience.' Rep is positioning AI as the CFO-level win theme - though no Changi stakeholder (Mun Wai, the CFO target) is yet quoted using AI language.
• Per the 27 Apr 2026 rep note: 'Not able to re-engage. Feedback from partner is the same. Changi became silent as working internally on RFP (expected by mid-year).' Per 13 Apr 2026: 'working internally on RFP to be delivered by June. Became silent.' Buyer has gone quiet; AI angle is rep-side strategy without buyer confirmation.
• Medium confidence: Product scope is mixed traditional + AI (iContract;iSource;ML iAnalyze;iSupplier;eProcurement;eInvoicing;iSaaS;iRisk - notably no Merlin/Agentic/ANA in this opp's product list). KPMG/Accenture-partner-led. Avoma returned 0 meetings. Rep explicitly names 'superior AI capabilities' as the engagement strategy, but absent named-buyer corroboration this stays AI Curious.

contacts.name: Yuh Khee Leong
contacts.title: Senior Vice President, Corporate IT & Technology

items.quote: no Avoma calls found for this opp
Action to engage with CFO based on superior AI capabilities
Mun Wai Mak is not favoring Zycus, but is open to meet
evaluation has slowed while ME situation improves

metrics.narrative: No quantified value case exists anywhere in Salesforce or Next Step history and there are no Avoma calls to mine for buyer-stated numbers. The Feb 04 2026 Next Step frames the pitch around superior AI capabilities and mitigating the previous experience, but no cost, cycle-time, headcount, savings, or compliance figure has ever been articulated by a Changi stakeholder. The deal remains qualitative and tied to the broader S4 HANA migration story. Quantifying an AI-led sourcing and contract-intelligence ROI with Mak Mun Wai (Vice President Finance) is the move that would give the CFO a reason to prioritize the evaluation.

champion.narrative: There is no champion. The economic buyer, Mak Mun Wai, is cautious and not favoring Zycus, so he cannot carry the deal internally. Procurement leaders (Ng Ching, Associate General Manager; Nursakina Noorani and Chua Lay Ling in procurement) and Corporate IT (Yuh Khee Leong, Senior Vice President) are plausible champion candidates who engaged positively at the Feb 04 2026 meeting, but none shows advocacy behavior: no internal selling, no RFP-criteria access, no pushing back on rivals for us. The buyer going silent since February with only Could Not Connect outbound attempts underlines the single-threaded, championless state. Cultivating one senior procurement or IT sponsor is the highest-priority relationship move.

competition.narrative: Four rivals are named in Competitors__c: SAP Ariba, GEP Worldwide, iValua and Coupa. SAP Ariba is the strongest current threat because Changi's S4 HANA migration is on track (Accenture, 3 Mar 2026), giving Ariba a native-ERP integration argument the others lack. No Avoma call quotes a buyer preference for any vendor, so the ranking rests on the ERP-integration logic plus the standing field, not on a stated buyer lean. Zycus's wedge is agentic AI differentiation and the STE and NTUC reference wins to overcome the past-project trust gap; that narrative must land with the CFO before the RFP is written.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 24 Nov 2024, 3 Mar 2026, 2 Dec 2024, 12 Sep 2024
identify_pain.narrative: The pain is inferred rather than buyer-stated: procurement transformation is being pulled along by the S4 HANA ERP migration (Nov 24 2024, main focus is S4H migration; 3 Mar 2026, SAP migration now on track), and the 2 Dec 2024 invitation to SIs to share Gen AI use cases signals appetite to modernize sourcing with AI. A distinct, disqualifying element is the reputational overhang from a past Zycus project (12 Sep 2024, not well positioned because of past project) that colors the whole evaluation. No pain owner has quantified the operational cost of the status quo. A discovery session with procurement and IT to name and size the specific pain Zycus AI addresses is the move.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal, procurement channel, InfoSec) have surfaced, which is expected for a Qualified deal with no RFP yet issued. No Current Contract Expiration is populated, so there is no incumbent-renewal forcing function. As a Singapore aviation enterprise undergoing an S4 HANA migration, Changi will almost certainly require InfoSec review and ERP-integration scoping, but none of that is visible yet. Visibility only comes once the RFP lands and a procurement channel is named.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Mak Mun Wai, Vice President Finance
economic_buyer.narrative: Mak Mun Wai, Vice President Finance, is the identified economic buyer: he sits on the OpportunityContactRole and is the named CFO target across the Next Step history and nearly every 2026 outbound touch. Authority is clear but sponsorship is not: the 16 Sep 2024 note states he is not favoring Zycus due to a past project, and the Feb 04 2026 action is still to engage the CFO to get executive buying, meaning he has not been won. Recent contact is entirely one-way (1:1 email 27 Apr and 4 May 2026, Horizon invite 15 Jun 2026, outbound calls 26/29 May marked Could Not Connect), so there is no live two-way EB engagement. Converting him from cautious gatekeeper to sponsor, using the STE and NTUC reference wins to rebuild trust, is the central EB task.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a partner-influenced, RFP-led selection tied to its S4 HANA ERP migration: Accenture confirmed CAG should go to market in June/July as the SAP migration is on track (3 Mar 2026) and KPMG has repeatedly reconfirmed the evaluation timing (16 Sep 2024). Procurement (Ng Ching, Nursakina Noorani, Chua Lay Ling), Finance (Mak Mun Wai) and Corporate IT (Yuh Khee Leong) all appear in the org picture, indicating a multi-function committee. What is unknown is the internal approval chain and who owns the RFP; no Salesforce process field or call confirms it. Getting the RFP timeline and decision owners in writing from the partners is the way to firm this up.

decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria have been documented because the RFP has not been released; the buyer is described as working internally on it (13/27 Apr 2026). The only directional signals are the rep-asserted emphasis on superior AI capabilities (4 Feb 2026) and the 2 Dec 2024 note that SIs were invited to share Gen AI use cases, suggesting AI-led innovation will feature in the eventual criteria. Nothing is buyer-confirmed. Securing early sight of the RFP requirements, ideally through KPMG or Accenture, is what would turn this from a gap into a scored element.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — Mak Mun Wai is not favoring Zycus due to a past project (Next Step 16 Sep 2024); buyer silent since Feb 2026 with no Zycus-specific pull.

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS_Score__c 5.0 with AIS_Status__c Moderate. The buyer's Gen AI interest is real at the program level (SIs invited to share Gen AI use cases, 2 Dec 2024) but the appetite is rep-asserted, not buyer-confirmed, and no Avoma call exists to validate it.

bullets.full: No real champion yet — No champion. The economic buyer is cautious and championing no one internally, and no procurement or IT contact has shown advocacy behavior. Ng Ching and Nursakina Noorani are the most plausible candidates to develop, but both need to be re-engaged from a five-month silence first.
bullets.text: No real champion yet — No champion.

The Feb 04 2026 Next Step frames the pitch around superior AI capabilities and mitigating the previous experience, but no cost, cycle-time, headcount, savings, or compliance figure has ever been articulated by a Changi stakeholder. The deal remains qualitative and tied to the broader S4 HANA migration story. Quantifying an AI-led sourcing and contract-intelligence ROI with Mak Mun Wai (Vice President Finance) is the move that would give the CFO a reason to prioritize the evaluation.

bullets.full: Edge over the competition — Recency-weighted, SAP Ariba is the single strongest current threat: Changi's S4 HANA migration is on track (Accenture, 3 Mar 2026), giving Ariba a native-ERP integration advantage, and no buyer has stated a preference for anyone else. GEP Worldwide, iValua and Coupa remain on the standing competitor field from deal creation with no fresh buyer signal, so they rank below Ariba as watch items. No competitor is buyer-preferred on the evidence; Zycus's real drag is the reputational overhang from a past project, not a named rival's lead. The wedge is agentic AI differentiation plus STE and NTUC references to rebuild trust before the RFP is written.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Changi Airport Group (Singapore) Pte. Ltd — Qualified · $550K · closes 25 Sep

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 13 is carried by momentum 55 vs stage-expected 50; exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: preference strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 55 (moving forward): Next Step carries 10 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (13), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 10 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c 5.0 with AIS_Status__c Moderate. The buyer's Gen AI interest is real at the program level (SIs invited to share Gen AI use cases, 2 Dec 2024) but the appetite is rep-asserted, not buyer-confirmed, and no Avoma call exists to validate it.

business_case.evidence: No ROI, savings, or AI impact quantified by the buyer; pitch is qualitative around superior AI capabilities.

Next Step reports evaluation has slowed while macro-environment situation improves

Close date pulled in from 20 Nov 2026 to 25 Sep 2026
Positive meeting with IT and procurement; strong possibility of shortlist, RFP in H2 2026

deal_movement.summary: Momentum is weakening. The last two-way buyer engagement was the 4 Feb 2026 positive meeting; every touch since is rep-side outbound with no reply. In 2026 the amount was cut, the close pulled in, the owner changed, and Next Step reports the evaluation has slowed, all pointing to a cooling, championless deal that has gone quiet ahead of an unreleased RFP.

SAP Ariba is the live threat because Changi's S4 HANA migration is on track, handing Ariba a native-integration argument no rival can match.
There is no quantified value case and the finance chief, Mak Mun Wai, remains cautious after a prior project, so the price has no ROI anchor yet.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling to dark: the last two-way buyer touch was 4 Feb 2026, and 2026 outbound calls are marked Could Not Connect with no email replies. The deal is quiet, single-threaded, and awaiting a slipped RFP.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 29 Nov 2024, qualified 16 Sep 2025, 84 days to close (25 Sep 2026). Roughly 10 months in Qualified with no submission-date fields populated and no formal RFP, shortlist, or evaluation started. Field history shows the amount cut 750k to 550k (6 Jun 2026), close pulled in 20 Nov 2026 to 25 Sep 2026 (3 Mar 2026) and the owner changed to Carl Kimball (6 Jun 2026). Every 2026 buyer touch since February is rep-side outbound (Could Not Connect / email sent) with no two-way reply, and Next Step 6 Jun 2026 reports evaluation slowed. Buyer cold well beyond 60 days forces Off Track; the deal is not dead (RFP still expected) but momentum must be rebuilt.
evidence: Next Step 6 Jun 2026: evaluation has slowed while ME (macro-environment) situation improves
Next Step 27 Apr 2026: Not able to re-engage. Changi became silent as working internally on RFP (expected by mid-year)
Next Step 13 Apr 2026: working internally on RFP to be delivered by June. Became silent.

Next Step 4 Feb 2026: Positive meeting with strong possibility to be part of short list (RFP in second half of 2026). Action to engage with CFO to get executive buying based on superior AI capabilities. Still need to work on mitigating previous experience.
Next Step 16 Sep 2024: Key stakeholder Mun Wai Mak is not favoring Zycus, but is open to meet and hear proposition
Field history 6 Jun 2026: Amount 750000 to 550000 (by Carl Kimball); Owner Guillaume Pasquet to Carl Kimball
north_star_verdict.headline: Buyer silent since the 4 Feb 2026 positive meeting; the promised mid-year RFP never landed and evaluation has slowed, so this Qualified deal needs a full re-engagement before any 25 Sep close is credible.

flags: Rebuild trust before the RFP: line up STE and NTUC reference proof points to counter the past-project overhang with the relevant stakeholder Wai.
Neutralize SAP Ariba's S4 HANA-native pitch with a concrete Merlin agentic-AI integration story tied to Changi's migration.
Break single-thread reliance on a cautious economic buyer: cultivate Ng Ching or Nursakina Noorani in procurement as a champion who can access RFP criteria.
best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers matter most now: restart the dormant relationship, neutralize SAP Ariba's ERP-integration edge with a CFO-grade AI case, and break the single-thread reliance on a cautious economic buyer.

customer_preference.evidence: Mak Mun Wai is not favoring Zycus due to a past project (Next Step 16 Sep 2024); buyer silent since Feb 2026 with no Zycus-specific pull.

competitive_position.summary: Recency-weighted, SAP Ariba is the single strongest current threat: Changi's S4 HANA migration is on track (Accenture, 3 Mar 2026), giving Ariba a native-ERP integration advantage, and no buyer has stated a preference for anyone else. GEP Worldwide, iValua and Coupa remain on the standing competitor field from deal creation with no fresh buyer signal, so they rank below Ariba as watch items. No competitor is buyer-preferred on the evidence; Zycus's real drag is the reputational overhang from a past project, not a named rival's lead. The wedge is agentic AI differentiation plus STE and NTUC references to rebuild trust before the RFP is written.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Accenture confirms CAG should go to market in June/July (as SAP migration is now on track)
competitors.how_we_win: Counter native-ERP lock-in with an agentic AI value story (Merlin sourcing/contract intelligence) for CFO Mak Mun Wai, backed by STE and NTUC references.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.how_we_win: Position depth of AI-native source-to-pay and reference wins against GEP's managed-services framing.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with faster time-to-value and the Merlin agentic differentiation.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Emphasize AI-led sourcing and total-cost fit versus Coupa's premium pricing.

items.deliverable: Confirm the S2P RFP release date and whether procurement is in scope of the S4 HANA program
items.grounding_quote: working internally on RFP to be delivered by June. Became silent.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is the rep's declared win theme (superior AI capabilities, 4 Feb 2026) but it is under-positioned: no Changi stakeholder has been quoted using AI language, the product scope carries no Merlin/agentic module, and there is no buyer call to confirm appetite. The narrative needs to be put in front of the CFO with a concrete agentic use case.
